memory_recovered: Operational state is not contained in one file. ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a retained canonical snapshot, while append-only queue updates form a fresher event ledger. Effective work selection requires a derived view that preserves both layers.

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json predates multiple later queue-update commits.
Later updates may add queue items or change status without rewriting the canonical snapshot.
inferred: Reading only the canonical snapshot can cause duplicate work or stale blocker selection.
proposed: Use the committed reconciler to derive an effective queue before each team selects work.
superseded: Treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as a complete current-state authority.
unresolved: Repository queue-update records do not consistently include an explicit updated_at field or immutable source SHA inside the document.

ordering_rule: Valid timestamp ascending, then source path lexicographically, then caller input order.
conflict_rule: Materially different updates for the same item and timestamp are retained in provenance and surfaced as a conflict. The deterministic derived value does not erase the conflict.

verification.method: deterministic Node test contract committed with the implementation
scenarios: newer status overrides older status in the derived view
new queue item is incorporated without mutating the canonical input
same-time materially different updates produce an explicit conflict
verification.runtime_status: test source committed; no GitHub Actions execution evidence was available during this run

falsification_or_review_route: Run the test under a supported Node runtime, then execute the CLI against ACTIVE_QUEUE.json and all operations/queue-updates/*.json records. Revise the contract if ordering is nondeterministic, a source artifact is mutated, or a materially conflicting concurrent update is not reported.
next_action: Wire the reconciler into a read-only effective-queue generation check and require queue updates to carry explicit updated_at and source identity fields.
